domain: Aboriginal Wandjina rain-spirit cave paintings (Kimberley NW Australia; depicted with eyes+nose but NO MOUTH; ritually silent — Rainbow Serpent sealed mouths to prevent endless rain)
specific_mechanism: Output-head-only surface-form suppression fine-tuning with explicit hidden-state preservation loss; null emission (not refusal substitution).
llm_application: WANDJINA-MOUTHLESS-FT: a surgical fine-tuning that suppresses generation of a target concept's surface forms while strictly preserving internal hidden-state representations. Loss = α * NLL(null_token | x, target_concept) + β * MSE(h_intermediate, h_reference_intermediate) where h_reference is the un-suppressed model's hidden states. Only the LM head is updated; intermediate layers are frozen with hidden-state regularizer. Output is empty/null token, NOT a refusal phrase. Differs from refusal training (substitutes refusal text), unlearning (destroys representations), output filtering (post-hoc deletion), Just Enough Shifts (selective shifts both internal+external) by combining (a) output-head-only suppression, (b) explicit intermediate-layer freeze + hidden-state preservation, (c) null-token emission instead of refusal substitute.
novelty_claim: Targeted Representation FT (Just Enough Shifts ICLR 2026), Steering the CensorShip activation-vector censorship, Programming Refusal ICLR 2025 already cover surgical output control. WANDJINA-MOUTHLESS-FT recombines output-head-only + hidden-state freeze + null-token emission. Small extension of existing surgical-suppression literature.
